AADSTS50001.message: Resource not found in the tenant.
AADSTS50001.description: This error occurs when the resource identifier (application ID or URI) is missing from the registered applications in the tenant.
AADSTS50001.resolution: Ask your tenant admin to register the application or consent to it. Alternatively, ensure you are signing in to the correct tenant.

AADSTS50011.message: The reply URL specified in the request does not match.
AADSTS50011.description: The redirect URI sent in the request does not match any of the redirect URIs registered for the application.
AADSTS50011.resolution: Ensure the redirect URI in your authentication request matches one of the redirect URIs configured in the app registration in the Azure portal.

AADSTS50020.message: User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant.
AADSTS50020.description: The user's account from the identity provider is not present in this tenant. B2B collaboration may be needed.
AADSTS50020.resolution: Invite the user to the tenant using Azure AD B2B collaboration, or verify the user exists in the correct tenant.

AADSTS65001.message: The user or administrator has not consented to use the application.
AADSTS65001.description: A consent error — the application requires permissions that the user or admin has not granted.
AADSTS65001.resolution: Have the user consent to the application permissions, or have a tenant admin grant admin consent in the Azure portal.

AADSTS70001.description: The application has been disabled in the tenant by an administrator.

AADSTS700016.message: The application was not found in the directory/tenant.
AADSTS700016.description: The application with the given identifier was not found in the directory. Check that you are using the correct application ID and signing in to the correct tenant.
AADSTS700016.resolution: Verify the application (client) ID is correct and that the app is registered in the tenant you're signing in to.
